sections.heading: 1. The Core Components
sections.content: To use reflection, you need the kotlin-reflect library. The API revolves around three main classes: KClass, KFunction, and KProperty.

sections.heading: 2. Introspection in Action
sections.content: Reflection allows you to inspect a class structure at runtime.

sections.heading: Class Introspection Example
3. Dynamic Invocation
sections.content: You can call functions dynamically using KFunction references.

sections.heading: Dynamic Function Call Example
sections.code:
class Greeter {
    fun sayHello(name: String) = "Hello, $name!"
}

val greeter = Greeter()
val function = Greeter::sayHello

val result = function.call(greeter, "Android Developer")
println(result)

sections.heading: 4. Annotations & Metadata
sections.content: Annotations provide metadata that can be read at runtime using reflection. Retention must be set to RUNTIME.

sections.heading: Annotation Reflection Example
sections.content: Reading annotation metadata from a property.
sections.code:
@Target(AnnotationTarget.PROPERTY)
@Retention(AnnotationRetention.RUNTIME)
annotation class JsonKey(val name: String)

data class User(@JsonKey("user_full_name") val name: String)

val property = User::name
val annotation = property.annotations.find { it is JsonKey } as? JsonKey
println(annotation?.name)

sections.heading: 5. Reflection vs Code Generation (KSP)
sections.content: Reflection works at runtime and is flexible but slower. Code generation (KSP/Kapt) works at compile time and generates optimized code.

sections.heading: Performance Comparison
items: Reflection: Runtime, flexible, slower, higher memory usage.
Code Generation (KSP/Kapt): Compile-time, faster, better for production apps.
Prefer code generation for performance-critical Android features.

sections.heading: 6. Reified Types Connection
sections.content: Inline functions with reified types allow access to generic type information at runtime, which normally isn't possible due to type erasure.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Reflection inspects and manipulates code structure at runtime.
KClass, KFunction, and KProperty are the main reflection tools.
Annotations provide metadata readable via reflection.
Reflection can impact performance; prefer KSP for production.
Reified types partially overcome generic type erasure.
